description: Trace a polygon around Bora Bora's main volcanic island (16.5004°S, 151.7415°W), excluding surrounding reef and motu islets. Measure the area (sq km) and perimeter (km), then calculate the Polsby-Popper compactness score: (4 * pi * Area) / (Perimeter^2). Save the polygon as 'Bora Bora Main Island' in My Places. Write measurements to /home/ga/bora_bora_shape_analysis.txt in the format: Location, Area, Perimeter, Polsby-Popper Score.

metadata.data_source: Google Earth satellite imagery - real geographic data
